name: West Ukrainian People's Republic (1918–1919)

colors.symbolism: the historic azure of the medieval Galician arms
the heraldic gold of Galicia and the wheat fields of the region

description: A horizontal bicolour — blue over yellow — identical in design to the modern Ukrainian national flag.
history: The West Ukrainian People's Republic (Zakhidno-Ukrayinska Narodna Respublyka, ZUNR) was a short-lived state in eastern Galicia, declared on 1 November 1918 with its capital in Lviv. The republic united with the Ukrainian People's Republic by Act of Zluky on 22 January 1919 but was overrun by Polish forces by mid-1919. Its flag, codified in November 1918, was the blue-over-yellow bicolour — the heraldic colours of the medieval Kingdom of Galicia–Volhynia — which is the direct ancestor of the modern Ukrainian flag.
